title: prunella × codinae
intro: Prunella × codinae, also known as hybrid selfheal, is a perennial plant belonging to the mint family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been found to be effective against various bacterial and viral infections. The plant is rich in essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Prunella × codinae can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers are also used in herbal preparations, including infusions, salves, and creams, to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
